Title: Thai police probe weapons cache at Chinese man's home
Article:
BANGKOK: Thai police are investigating a vast arsenal of military-grade weapons – including assault rifles, explosives, grenades and anti-personnel mines – found at the home of a Chinese man, authorities said today.
Two M16 assault rifles, hundreds of rounds of ammunition, Russian landmines, projectiles, gasoline and nearly five kilos (11 pounds) of explosives were found at the home of Sun Mingchen, 31, near the beach resort of Pattaya.
There were no clear links to a planned attack, Chonburi province's public relations department said in a statement, but local police chief Pongphan Wongmanithet told reporters at the scene they were "investigating security implications."
Sun was charged with illegal possession of unauthorised weapons and could face up to 10 years in prison.
Thai media reported that the suspect's phone contained AI chatbot searches on military plastic explosive properties and videos of him handling the weapons, fuelling concerns of possible "terrorist" intent.
Police are looking into the motive of the suspect, who will be taken for psychological evaluation, Pongphan said.
The raid on Sun's home was prompted after police found a pistol in a car with two Chinese nationals on board during a traffic stop on Friday.
According to authorities, he held a long-term visa in Thailand and had lived in the property for about two years. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bintulu Port handover process at final documentation stage - Loke
Article:
KOTA SAMARAHAN: The process of handing over Bintulu Port to the Sarawak State Government is expected to be completed within the next few months after all key issues have been finalised, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
He said a final meeting with Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas will be held soon to finalise the matters.
"Currently, the final agreement is being finalised, as legal and pricing issues have already been resolved.
"As I announced earlier, the federal and the state governments have reached a consensus on the final cost of the handover," he told a press conference after launching a new route under the BAS.MY Kuching Stage Bus Service Transformation Programme here today.
He said the draft final agreement has been completed and is now awaiting Cabinet approval before the official handover can take place, adding that the process has taken time as the relevant act on the dissolution of the Bintulu Port Authority was passed about two years ago.
He said the enforcement of the authority's dissolution and the official handover to the state government remain subject to the signing of the final agreement.
The Federal Government and the Sarawak State Government had, in principle, agreed on a value of RM1.8 billion for the state's acquisition of Bintulu Port.
Meanwhile, Loke said the Ministry of Economy has approved a RM445 million project to upgrade the Miri Airport building under Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), last month.
He said the upgrading works are expected to begin this year following approval of the cost based on the value assessment review session held on March 17.
"The expansion of Miri Airport is important as it is the second busiest airport in Sarawak after Kuching International Airport. Besides being a key domestic airport, it also serves as a major hub for Rural Air Services (RAS) operated by AirBorneo," he said.
He said the service plays a crucial role in connecting Sarawak's interior areas with major towns and improving air transport accessibility for rural communities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno's 80th anniversary a launchpad for the next 20 years, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno's 80th anniversary is not merely a commemoration of its 1946 origins but a milestone to shape the party's direction for the next two decades, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said the party's eight decades in the political landscape should serve as a period of reflection to strengthen strategic planning and produce capable leaders to ensure continuity as Umno approaches its centenary in 2046.
"The experience gained over these 80 years should serve as guidance in preparing agendas for the next 20 years and beyond," he said in a special interview held in conjunction with the anniversary.
Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, said this year's celebration was meaningful due to the success of several major initiatives, including the Rumah Bangsa programme and the National Collaboration effort involving professionals and influencers.
"The Cabinet has agreed for the Ummah Consultative Council to translate these agendas into action. This represents a national agenda where there should be no differences in opinion regarding collectively agreed items," he said.
Reflecting on the party's decline after the 14th General Election (GE14) in 2018, the deputy prime minister admitted Umno had faced a serious crisis of public confidence.
"Umno experiences a crisis almost every 10 years, and 2018 was a crisis of declining public confidence.
"For the next 20 years, I have stressed the need to develop a new generation of leaders. I must first lay a strong foundation before a capable line-up can steer Umno towards its 100th anniversary," he said.
He added that efforts to rebuild the party are being carried out gradually, focusing on internal unity and reclaiming grassroots support.
"I am not saying that Umno is already strong, but we are gradually organising programmes so we can return to our original footing, with the support of the Malays forming the basis of this process," he said.
Umno supreme council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan recently reported that the party's membership stood at 3.525 million as of January this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aeronas sues Cape Manufacturing for RM279.5mil over aircraft deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cape Manufacturing (M) Sdn Bhd (Cape), a subsidiary of Cape EMS Bhd, is facing a RM279.5 million lawsuit by Aeronas Aerokraf Sdn Bhd (Aeronas) for breach of contract.
Aeronas, in a statement, said the writ and statement of claim were filed at the Johor Baru High Court.
The civil suit filed by Aeronas against Cape is based on an alleged breach of agreement, whereby Aeronas claims that Cape failed to fulfil its contractual obligations under the agreement dated March 9, 2025, namely to carry out a capital injection and provide a loan to Aeronas amounting to more than RM50 million.
Aeronas owns the licensing and exclusive rights from Mael Aircraft Corporation to manufacture and commercialise the Model BA-42 twin-engine turboprop six-passenger aircraft.
Aeronas subsequently entered into an agreement with Cape for a joint venture aimed at realising the project of making Aeronas the first aircraft manufacturer in Malaysia to manufacture and commercialise the Aircraft.
According to the statement of claim filed by Aeronas, Cape's failure to fulfil its contractual obligation to undertake the capital injection caused the launch of the Aircraft, which was scheduled for December 2025, to be delayed.
"Aeronas is also at risk of losing orders from its existing customers who had ordered the Aircraft due to the delay."
The High Court of Johor Baru fixed May 25 for the first case management for the civil suit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Umno, BN comfortable in Unity Government, focus remains on people
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) are comfortable working with all component parties in the Unity Government as they share common objectives in serving the people, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said the current cooperation is the result of a strong understanding among government parties to ensure the public is not burdened by political polemics.
"The people must not be troubled by prolonged polemics and differences in stance or views," the deputy prime minister said in a special interview held in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary recently.
Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said all parties in the Unity Government have agreed to set aside political differences to ensure the effective implementation of the 15th General Election (GE15) manifestos.
He said the government prioritised common ground found in the various manifestos, particularly concerning the cost of living, education, and unemployment.
Zahid, who is also the Rural and Regional Development Minister, noted that 47 per cent of the manifestos from the component parties contained shared elements, forming a solid foundation for cooperation.
"Why should we be concerned about differences when we share the same goal?
"In addition, there are detailed issues we must address. That is why we have the Inter-Party Consultative Council to resolve differences and ensure the manifesto is implemented with clear purpose," he said.
He also emphasised that all component parties, including DAP, uphold the four main pillars enshrined in the Federal Constitution.
"This is evident in the comfort that has developed and the strong respect shown towards the agreement to form the Unity Government, which is founded on four main pillars: Islam, the Malay Rulers, the Malay language, and the special rights of the Malays.
"They not only respect it but also acknowledge that this is a fact enshrined in the Constitution," he said.
The Unity Government was formed after GE15 in 2022 when no coalition secured a simple majority. It is led by Pakatan Harapan alongside BN, Gabungan Parti Sarawak, Gabungan Rakyat Sabah, and Parti Warisan, with support from several other parties and independent members of parliament.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Top Sri Lankan Buddhist monk arrested over alleged sex abuse
Article:
COLOMBO: Sri Lankan authorities arrested a senior Buddhist monk on Saturday for the alleged sexual abuse of an underage girl, police said, marking the highest-profile case involving clergy in the country.
Pallegama Hemarathana, 71, was arrested at a private hospital in the capital Colombo where he had sought treatment over the weekend amid an investigation into the alleged abuse of the 11-year-old girl in 2022.
The crime is alleged to have taken place at a highly venerated temple in Anuradhapura, around 200 kilometres (125 miles) north of Colombo, where Hemarathana is the chief priest.
"We will be guided by the magistrate on further action," a police statement said Saturday.
The monk will soon be presented before a judge.
Authorities added that the victim's mother has also been arrested for aiding and abetting the monk.
The development comes a day after a local court in Anuradhapura imposed a foreign travel ban on the monk.
There have been several cases of clergy abusing children in Sri Lanka, but the latest arrest involves the most senior monk to be accused of such a crime.
The 71-year-old is the chief custodian of a tree believed to have been grown from a sapling of the Bodhi tree in India that sheltered the Buddha when he attained enlightenment more than 2,500 years ago.
He is also the chief of eight highly venerated temples that are on a key Buddhist pilgrim route.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nanta hints at leaving Kapit to make way for younger blood
Article:
KUCHING: Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi has hinted that he will not defend his Kapit parliamentary seat in the next general election, signaling an end to nearly 30 years of service at the federal level.
Nanta, 68, said the move is intended to make way for younger leaders to continue the party's struggle and bring fresh leadership to the constituency.
"My intention in stating this is to make way for the youth. I already have a plan for the next step, should I be needed," he said when met at the Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) supreme council meeting here today.
Nanta, who is also Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) secretary-general, did not rule out the possibility of continuing his political career at the state level if given the opportunity and the blessing of the party leadership.
"Perhaps if there is an opportunity in a new place... I would like to serve at the state level.
"I have been in Parliament for almost 30 years. If it is permitted by the party president (Tan Sri Abang Johari Abang Openg), I might want to continue my career in the state arena," he said.
He added that his political future remains subject to the final decision of the party leadership. He also hinted at the possibility of contesting in a new state constituency in Kapit following the redelineation exercise.
"Coincidentally, there is a new state seat there. I remain committed to serving the people wherever I am placed," he said.
Nanta, the grandson of legendary Dayak leader Tun Temenggong Jugah Barieng, has served as the Kapit member of parliament for 27 years since 1999.
He has held the seat for six terms, including winning uncontested in the 11th and 12th general elections.
He first joined the federal cabinet as deputy rural and regional development minister in 2013, before being appointed domestic trade and consumer affairs minister in 2021. He has served as the works minister since December 2022.
He also briefly served as acting entrepreneur development and cooperatives minister for two weeks from Dec 3 to 16, 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu suspension: Ketereh MP Khlir Mohd Nor 'not surprised' by decision
Article:
KOTA BARU: Ketereh member of parliament Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor, who was among five Bersatu MPs suspended by the party yesterday, said he accepted the decision with an open heart.
Khlir said he was not surprised by the disciplinary action taken against him and several other party leaders.
"I was not surprised by yesterday's decision," he said today.
He added that he had yet to decide whether to submit an appeal against the suspension.
"I received the letter yesterday morning, but I have not made any decision on whether to appeal or otherwise," he said.
Meanwhile, efforts to get comments from Bersatu Tanah Merah MP, Datuk Seri Ikmal Abdul Aziz proved futile.
Yesterday, Bersatu
suspended five MPs
for two party election terms over alleged violations of the party constitution and code of ethics.
Besides Khlir and Ikmal, the other three were Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin), Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut) and Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu).
Two Bersatu assemblymen, Dzowahir Ab Ghani (Suka Menanti) and Abdul Razak Khamis (Sungai Tiang), were also sacked.
The suspension, which took effect yesterday, was carried out under Clause 22.5 of the party constitution.
Bersatu Disciplinary Board chairman Datuk Mohd Radzi Manan, who signed the notices, said those suspended would face a two-term suspension from party elections, effective immediately.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drugs seized from Jordanian man and wife traced to 'Abe'
Article:
TUMPAT: Drugs found in the possession of a Jordanian man and his wife in a recent raid were supplied by an individual known as 'Abe', who is believed to be linked to a Thailand-based drug trafficking syndicate.
Authorities said the consignment, believed to comprise about six kilogrammes of cannabis buds worth an estimated RM460,000, was intended for distribution in Kuala Lumpur.
General Operations Force Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the syndicate had also instructed the group to hand over the drugs to a man known only as "Lee".
Preliminary investigations revealed that the three suspects — a 47-year-old Jordanian man, his wife and a 35-year-old local woman — admitted they were paid RM300 per kg to transport the drugs to the capital.
"Investigations showed the drugs were obtained from a dealer in Sungai Golok known as 'Abe'," he said.
Ahmad Radzi said the 35-year-old local woman is believed to have acted as a middleperson for the Thailand-based syndicate, while the Jordanian man and his wife were her accomplices in the operation.
He added that the trio collected the drugs from an illegal jetty near Masjid Taqwa in Kampung Simpangan.
"All three suspects claimed it was their first time using the illegal jetty to retrieve narcotics," he said.
The arrests were made during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan in Simpangan at about 4.30pm yesterday.
Police also seized a Mercedes-Benz valued at RM150,000, along with several other items, bringing total seizures to RM630,000.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B and Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Schools reach out to Canvas hackers as breach hits US classrooms
Article:
DETROIT: Some schools and universities whose students' data was stolen by a cybercriminal hacking group as part of an April breach of the educational tool Canvas have reached out to the hackers to prevent their data from being released, a source familiar with the matter said on Friday.
ShinyHunters, a hacking group with a string of data theft and extortion campaigns targeting major global companies, said in a May 3 post on its website that it had stolen roughly 6.65 terabytes of Canvas data related to nearly 9,000 schools worldwide that included student names, email addresses and private messages between students, teachers and other staff.
Student newspapers across the country reported this week the hack was causing widespread disruption as students prepare for end-of-year tasks and assignments. The software is used by schools for class assignments and information sharing, as well as messages between students and school faculty.
The FBI said on Friday it was aware of a breach disrupting the US education system, without naming Canvas.
On May 5, the group posted a message saying that Canvas' parent company, Instructure, had "not even bothered speaking to us" to prevent a data leak, and that their demand "was not even as high as you might think it is." The message included a list of roughly 1,400 individual schools and districts, and invited the schools to contact them to negotiate and prevent data from being posted.
Instructure announced in a May 1 post on its support website that it was investigating a cybersecurity incident. A post the next day, signed by Chief Information Security Officer Steve Proud, said the "information involved" included Canvas user names, email addresses, student ID numbers and messages among users. In a May 6 update, the company said the situation was resolved and that Canvas was fully operational.
On May 7, students at multiple schools reported attempting to log into Canvas and finding a note from ShinyHunters with a link to the list of affected schools. Instructure pulled Canvas, Canvas Beta and Canvas Test offline a short time later, but restored access to Canvas four hours later.
An Instructure spokesperson said in an email Friday that the hackers "made changes to pages that appeared when some students and teachers were logged in."
The hackers exploited an issue related to the company's Free-for-Teacher service, the spokesperson said, which allows non-Canvas users to try certain parts of the platform. The company has temporarily shut down the Free-for-Teacher service, which "gives us confidence to restore access to Canvas, which is now fully back online and available for use," the spokesperson said.
Canvas Beta and Canvas Test remain in "maintenance mode," according to Instructure's support site.
ShinyHunters pulled both messages off its website as of May 7, replacing them with a message saying they were "not commenting and have no further comment to make regarding this global incident." A group representative declined to answer questions from Reuters sent via online chat.
Extortion and ransomware groups pull claims about victims off their websites for any number of reasons, including sometimes that a target has paid or is in negotiations.
A note sent to parents from the South Orange-Maplewood School District Friday said the security breach occurred April 25 and that Instructure detected unauthorised activity April 29.
Montgomery County Public Schools in Maryland told students, staff and families in an email Friday that Canvas was returning to service, but that the district was continuing to restrict access out of an abundance of caution "until all services have been reviewed and confirmed safe for use."
Canvas has 30 million active users between kindergarten and college age, according to Instructure's website.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire Dept marks first locally bred K9 detection dog with Labrador pup 'Brave'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Fire and Rescue Department has achieved a milestone with the birth of its first locally bred K9 unit detection dog.
Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the male Labrador puppy, named Brave, was born on April 5 at the department's K9 Unit Operational Training Centre in Bertam, Penang.
"A Labrador under the supervision of fire officer Nelson Karupak, named Lottie, successfully gave birth to the puppy.
"I have named him 'Brave' with the hope that he will become a courageous and strong four-legged hero in rescue operations," he said at the 2026 World Firefighters Day Carnival here today.
Nga said the successful local breeding could significantly reduce costs, as importing a trained detection dog from Birmingham in the United Kingdom typically costs about RM150,000 per animal.
[[nid:1435643]]
"Previously, our K9 units were sourced from breeding centres in Birmingham, where they were raised and trained before being sent to Malaysia.
"My target is for our fire department to be capable of breeding and training our own local K9 units. My vision is that one day, we can export them back to the United Kingdom and to Asean nations such as Singapore, Thailand, the Philippines, and Indonesia," he said.
Recognising the importance of the unit, Nga added that the government had previously approved RM2.4 million for the procurement of 16 new detection dogs from the UK.
Currently, the department has 34 detection dogs but requires another 56 to reach its optimum operational capacity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rapid KL launches 'Ask Me' initiative for better travel experience
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid KL has launched the "Ask Me" customer assistance initiative at the Pasar Seni Bus Hub to provide commuters with faster and more accessible travel information.
Pasar Seni is the first location to implement the initiative, which aims to enhance the public transport experience through a dedicated service podium.
Rapid KL said in a statement today that the initiative helps passengers obtain travel assistance more easily, particularly at high-traffic hubs with multiple route connections.
"Passengers can obtain information on bus routes, stop locations, route changes, and travel planning, as well as travel pass products to facilitate their daily journeys.
"This initiative is part of the Service Ambassador 2.0 Programme, which features customer service assistants and traffic coordinators as the main personnel at key hotspots and major transport hubs," the statement said.
Rapid Bus acting chief executive officer Ku Jamil Zakaria said the initiative reflects Rapid KL's commitment to making public transport more responsive and reliable.
He said the Pasar Seni Bus Hub was selected as the pioneer site because it is a primary Klang Valley hub, handling 14 Rapid KL bus routes with an average of more than 6,000 passengers daily.
The "Ask Me" service will be expanded in stages to other locations, including the Weld Quay Bus Terminal for Rapid Penang services.
"We want to ensure passengers can obtain assistance more quickly and easily, especially at key interchange points.
"The presence of easily identifiable personnel not only helps reduce confusion but also boosts confidence when using public transport," Ku Jamil said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 1,000-year-old Quran in Narathiwat among region's valuable Islamic artefacts
Article:
NARATHIWAT: A Quran manuscript believed to be more than 1,000 years old and displayed at the Museum of Islamic Cultural Heritage and Al-Quran Learning Centre here not only holds immense Islamic historical value, but also reflects the ingenuity of early civilisations in utilising natural organic materials to produce the sacred text.
Weighing about 50 kilogrammes, the manuscript was produced during the Abbasid era using 346 sheets made from animal skin and carbon-based ink derived from natural materials, making it one of the rare and valuable ancient Islamic artefacts preserved in the region.
Museum officer Nik Ilham Nik Yusup said the museum's oldest artefact was produced with exceptional craftsmanship using hundreds of processed animal skins to create durable sheets for Quranic writing.
"All the basic materials were specially sourced from Hadhramaut in Yemen," he told Bernama recently.
Beyond the use of natural materials, the manuscript also reflects the resourcefulness of early Muslim communities in using elements from their surroundings.
Written entirely by hand in ancient Kufi script, the manuscript used organic carbon ink produced from natural sources.
"People in the past used materials obtained directly from nature. A mixture of wood and date palm bark was burned to produce ink before being combined with several other organic materials from the forest," he said.
As the manuscript was produced entirely from ancient organic elements, preserving it requires meticulous conservation methods free from modern chemicals and solvents. Cleaning and restoration works are carried out every six months.
"The surface of these animal skin pages is extremely sensitive. The use of chemicals or alcohol-based liquids could cause the original ink to crack, while the texture of the sheets could also be damaged.
"That is why we only use organic preservation methods to maintain the value and authenticity of this treasure," said Nik Ilham.
He said the manuscript began its journey from Yemen to the Malay Archipelago during the era of early Islamic preacher Sheikh Jumadil Kubra.
According to him, the manuscript was preserved in Indonesia for over 500 years before being brought to Narathiwat about five years ago during the Covid-19 pandemic.
"To ensure this invaluable heritage of Islamic civilisation continues to be preserved, strict security procedures are implemented and visitors are not allowed to touch the artefact," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Army ramps up border enforcement to curb cross-border crimes, leakages
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The army has ramped up enforcement along the country's frontiers to clamp down on cross-border crimes and curb leakages.
Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said heightened controls and enforcement measures were being implemented in line with the current security situation and annual trends observed.
"Every border is being tightened further, especially along with the northern border between Malaysia and Thailand.
"As we can see, during situations like this and ahead of Hari Raya Aidiladha, there are usually many illegal activities.
"It has become a yearly routine for us to further tighten controls alongside ongoing operations already being carried out," he said when met after attending the Army Run 2026.
Recently, the army foiled an attempt to smuggle 20 cattle from Thailand through the Sungai Golok route, estimated to be worth RM225,000, during a border operation.
Previously, in his speech during the Army Day celebrations last month, Azhan said the army will continue to strengthen the deployment of its personnel and assets along the country's borders to ensure national border security remains safeguarded and under control.
Meanwhile, commenting on the Army Run, Azhan said that it is not just a sporting event, but instead a symbol of unity and manifestation of the spirit of victory that becomes a pulse army.
He said the program is able to be a platform for patriotism and strengthen the public's trust in the army, adding that the event received an overwhelming response.
"It exceeded its initial target of 5,000 participants, with about 6,000 people taking part.
"For the first time, we did not expect such a strong turnout.
"I feel this response reflects the spirit and support of the public for this event. Insyaallah, we will continue organising it in the years to come," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retiree loses RM900,000 life savings to YouTube investment scam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A retiree has lost his entire life savings of RM900,000 after falling victim to an online investment scam promoted through YouTube advertisements.
The case was highlighted by Kepong Member of Parliament Lim Lip Eng, who shared the victim's account as a warning against increasingly sophisticated financial fraud targeting retirees.
According to Lim, the victim was initially lured by professionally produced videos featuring purported "investment experts" promising high and easy returns.
The scam appeared convincing, with a legitimate-looking website and providing small initial payouts to gain the victim's trust.
"The scammers appeared credible, and the websites looked legitimate. At first, small returns were even credited into the man's account," Lim said.
"These fake profits gradually lowered his guard, making him believe he had found a safe and reliable investment opportunity."
However, as the man invested more funds, the returns stopped, and he eventually realised he had been deceived.
"RM900,000 vanished without a trace. A lifetime of sacrifice, savings and hard work disappeared in just a few clicks.
"He was not uneducated, nor was he a reckless gambler. He was simply one of thousands of victims deceived by highly skilled scammers who understand human nature and know exactly how to exploit trust, hope and fear," Lim added.
Lim warned that modern scam operations are becoming increasingly sophisticated, often posing as legitimate investment platforms on social media.
He urged the public to share the warning, particularly with elderly family members, stressing that a moment of misplaced trust can lead to devastating financial loss.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Long working hours among doctors require wider healthcare reforms, says MMA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's healthcare system must undergo broader reforms to address excessive working hours and improve conditions for doctors and healthcare workers, the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) said.
Its president Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo said reports of house officers working up to 85 hours a week were deeply concerning and posed risks not only to doctors' welfare but also to patient safety.
He said house officers are in hospitals to be trained as safe and competent doctors, but exhaustion compromises both their ability to learn and the quality of care provided to patients.
"The immediate priority is clear: the 60 to 62-hour weekly cap must be enforced, not just on paper. Hospital directors must be held accountable for this," he said in a statement.
Thirunavukarasu said the MMA welcomed the reminder by Health director-general Datuk Dr Mahathar Abu Wahab to hospital directors regarding the flexible working hours system for house officers.
However, he said the directive itself was not new, and its re-emphasis reflected concerns that it had not been consistently implemented at ground level.
"The fact that it needs to be re-emphasised tells us something important — it has not been consistently followed on the ground."
He also said hospitals must honour house officers' leave entitlements, including annual and sick leave, which he described as rights under existing regulations rather than privileges.
"Denying or substituting them is not acceptable."
Thirunavukarasu said the country must confront deeper systemic issues behind excessive working hours, including chronic doctor shortages, inadequate staffing ratios and longstanding resource constraints within the healthcare system.
"A 60-hour week is still a heavy load for any healthcare worker. MMA's position has been consistent — Malaysia must move towards a maximum of 48 hours, and eventually 45 hours.
"To get there, we must address the systemic issues that make long hours persist."
He added that while directives are important, meaningful reform would require wider systemic changes across the healthcare sector.
"Directives remind. Systems reform. We need both."
Earlier, the Health Ministry directed hospitals nationwide to ensure house officers, or junior medical officers, work no more than
60 to 62 hours
a week under the government's flexible working system.
In a circular dated May 8 and issued to hospital directors, the ministry said the move was part of efforts to safeguard the welfare and rest periods of medical graduates while ensuring uninterrupted patient care and quality clinical training.
The directive followed confusion among healthcare administrators regarding the interpretation of the General Principles Guidelines on the Standardisation of the Flexible Working System introduced in 2019.
On Thursday, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry would issue a new circular on
working hours
for junior medical officers to strengthen governance and address concerns over excessive workloads among house officers.
Dzulkefly said the circular would reinforce existing flexi-working arrangements for house officers following reports that some were working between 65 and 85 hours a week.
Malaysian Medics International (MMI) highlighted the heavy workloads faced by junior doctors in Malaysia, saying they were significantly higher than in other countries.
According to the international group led by medical students, although the Health Ministry's flexi-system caps house officers' working hours at between 65 and 75 hours a week, chronic understaffing routinely pushes actual working hours far beyond the limit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiping council ready to cooperate with authorities over elephant transfer claims
Article:
TAIPING: The Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) is ready to cooperate with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) following social media allegations surrounding an international elephant conservation programme.
The programme involved the transfer of three elephants, Dara, Amoi, and Kelat, from Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) to the Tennoji Zoo in Japan.
MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said the council welcomed any review or investigation to maintain transparency and integrity.
"As part of its commitment to transparency and integrity, MPT always welcomes and is prepared to cooperate with any authorities, including the MACC, should there be any need to conduct further reviews or investigations into the overall collaboration programme," he said in a statement today.
He said the agreement between the two zoos did not involve any commercial transactions or payments.
"There are no commercial elements involving the sale and purchase of animals or any form of payment between the two parties under this agreement.
"The use of the term B2B (Business to Business) is not in line with the spirit of the agreement that has been signed," he said.
He said the programme involving elephants Dara, Amoi and Kelat complied with conservation guidelines and all legal requirements under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716).
He added that the programme was also in line with the Malaysian Zoo Standards Guidelines (Zoo Management) issued by the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry (NRES).
He said all mandatory procedures and approvals from the relevant authorities had been obtained before the elephants were sent to Japan.
"Complete documentation, including the application letter from ZTNS for the elephant contribution, the application for a special permit to keep elephants, and the request for information to provide feedback to the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, the Japanese CITES Scientific Authority, had already received approval and feedback from the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) beforehand.
"ZTNS also submitted an export application to the Perhilitan, which was subsequently approved by the NRES before the elephants were sent to Japan.
"Representatives from MPT, ZTNS and the Perhilitan had also visited Osaka from Oct 6 to 10, 2022, to assess the suitability of facilities and identify improvements required for the conservation programme," he said.
He added that animal welfare and well-being remained the priority throughout the programme.
"MPT understands that the public reaction stems from compassion and concern for the welfare of the animals, particularly the three elephants that are close to the hearts of Malaysians," he said.
He said ZTNS would continue communicating with Tennoji Zoo and relevant agencies to ensure proper standards of care were maintained.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three rescued, 10 houses submerged in Sungai Siput flash floods
Article:
IPOH: More than 10 houses in Kampung Lasah, Sungai Siput, were submerged in metre-deep floodwaters early today following four hours of continuous heavy rain.
The Sungai Siput Civil Defence Force (APM) operations control centre said several surrounding roads, including those in Kampung Chenein, Kampung Jong, and Kampung Bawong, were also inundated.
"We received a distress call at 1.24am. The heavy rain lasted four hours, but by 6am it had eased to a drizzle.
"Roads were flooded and access routes were cut off. Our personnel are on-site monitoring the situation. Conditions are currently under control," the statement said.
As of 9am, no temporary relief centres had been opened, though some residents moved to the homes of neighbours or relatives. Weather conditions have since improved.
Meanwhile, Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said three people, including an elderly woman and a woman with disabilities, were rescued after being trapped in their home.
He said the department received a call at 4.13am. The flooding was caused by Sungai Pelus overflowing, with water levels reaching an estimated one metre by the time rescuers arrived.
"We rescued an 80-year-old woman, a 55-year-old woman with disabilities, and a man in his 40s. The operations chief advised other villagers to evacuate if water levels fluctuate," Sabarodzi said.
The victims were moved to the Kampung Lasah community hall with the assistance of village committee members.
Sabarodzi added that water levels have begun to recede and the operation ended at 9.50am, though monitoring remains ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno opens 'Rumah Bangsa' doors to sacked, suspended Bersatu leaders
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is ready to accept leaders who were recently sacked or suspended by Bersatu under the Rumah Bangsa initiative.
The party's secretary-general, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, said Umno would welcome them unconditionally.
"Most of them came from Umno anyway. It would be like a homecoming," Asyraf said in a Facebook post.
Yesterday, Bersatu sacked two Kedah assemblymen and suspended seven other elected representatives over disciplinary breaches.
Those dismissed were Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani and Sungai Tiang assemblyman Abdul Razak Khamis.
Dzowahir is the state agriculture, plantation and transportation committee chairman, while Abdul Razak is a deputy state assembly speaker.
In March, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the party would welcome back former members who had previously been sacked or suspended, as well as those interested in joining Umno's struggle.
When contacted, Asyraf said Umno would welcome back those who were once part of the party.
"As our president said, we must embrace everyone who once belonged to Umno. As the saying goes, when you are on a journey and get lost, go back to the start.
"Hopefully, this will strengthen Umno and Rumah Bangsa," he said.
Asyraf added that Umno has recorded 17,000 new members under the Rumah Bangsa initiative over the past two weeks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trapped seafarers traumatised by Gulf fighting
Article:
LONDON: Isolated and traumatised by drones and missiles, seafarers in the Gulf face grave mental suffering after more than two months stuck on board in the Middle East war, maritime charities warn.
From captains to cooks, engineers and other officers, the workers who keep global freight flowing have found themselves not just stranded but in some cases right in the firing line of the US-Israeli war with Iran.
"We hear stories of how frightened they are. It's pretty scary," said Gavin Lim, head of the Crisis Response Network for the Sailors' Society, a UK-based seafarers' charity, who spoke with one crew whose vessel was hit. "They thought: 'We were going to die'."
Trade vessels have been struck by projectiles and fired on by Iranian Revolutionary Guards in dozens of incidents, according to the British maritime security monitor UKMTO.
At least 11 seafarers have been killed, according to the International Maritime Organization.
"They see drones flying, they see missiles flying, and then we see instances where the ships get hit," said Lim. "You can imagine that anxiety and fear building up. 'Are we just bait? Are we going to be a victim so that someone can make a point?'"
The Seafarers' Charity cites hypervigilance, burnout, fatigue, loneliness, depression and anxiety as some of the mental strains facing the 20,000 seafarers stranded by Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz since February 28.
At least two commercial vessels have been seized by Iranian forces under their blockade of the vital trade route. A video showed masked guards with guns boarding a ship.
"We heard that one of the seafarers, an officer, suffered a panic attack while the vessel was being boarded," said John Canias, maritime operations coordinator for the International Transport Workers' Federation (ITF).
"Fortunately, the news is they have been taken care of... they have been allowed to speak to their family through the internet."
Bereaved families
The strain extends to the seafarers' families at home, worried about their stranded loved ones – or in the worst cases, bereaved.
[[nid:1435605]]
On March 1, the second day of the conflict, a projectile hit a tanker in the Gulf, killing a 25-year-old Indian seafarer as he started his shift in the engine room, said Melanie Warman, communications director for the Sailors' Society, who spoke to his family.
"The mother has been in and out of hospital, not eating. It's obviously a really desperate situation," she told AFP.
"For the families, this is really, really difficult. We hear from families who can't reach their loved ones on board ships and they're really frantic with worry."
Like the Sailors' Society, another sailors' helpline charity, the International Seafarers' Welfare and Assistance Network (ISWAN), fields calls from trapped workers and offers them practical and psychological support.
"Most of the calls are around repatriation – what are their rights, how to go about it – and also obviously the stress and the worry about being in a conflict zone and not being trained or prepared for it," said ISWAN's chief executive, Simon Grainge.
Training to cope
Some charities are working with shipping companies to strengthen support for seafarers under unprecedented strain.
"The most up-to-date guidance we have on mental health and attacks is really based around Somali piracy, which is more under control" since attacks in the Indian Ocean surged in the early 2000s, said Deborah Layde, chief executive of the Seafarers' Charity.
"One of the things that quite a few organisations are now calling for is really up-to-date guidance on how to deal with wartime issues," she added. "This isn't something that a lot of shipping companies are ready for."
To that end, the charity has turned to mental health professionals to help provide guidelines and a webinar to guide seafarers to cope with the stress of the situation.
"There's this constant higher level of stress and hypervigilance without that ability to reset as they might normally do. There's exhaustion," said Rachel Glynn-Williams, a psychologist working with seafarers who is involved in developing the webinar.
"At the point I pick up crew conversations, they will have been on hyper-alert for a sustained amount of time, so their nervous system will be heightened and it's going to take a little time, depending on the individual, for that nervous system to reset," she told AFP.
"For some people, it might be fairly soon afterwards, within a matter of days, if not hours. For others, it might take a little longer."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man swept away in Kepong found dead after 3-day search
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A three-day search for a man believed to have fallen into Sungai Keruh in Kepong ended in tragedy when his body was discovered yesterday in a retention pond in Taman Sri Segambut.
The Kuala Lumpur Civil Defence Force said it received an emergency call regarding the incident at 5.27pm on Wednesday.
"A search and rescue (SAR) team from  Sentul was deployed to the location. The SAR operation took three days and covered Sungai Keruh, Sungai Gombak and Sungai Klang, involving a search radius of 16km.
"The victim's body was found on the third day of the SAR operation yesterday at a retention pond near Masjid Al-Qurtubi, Taman Sri Segambut," it told Harian Metro.
The Civil Defence Force said the victim was swept away and found about 4km from the location where he was believed to have fallen at Jalan Kuang Bulan, Kepong.
It added that the victim's identity could not be confirmed by the force and the body was handed over to police for further investigation.
It said the SAR operation was carried out with the cooperation of various agencies, including police, the Fire and Rescue Department, and Kuala Lumpur City Hall's rescue squad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't blame my wife, says father of boy crushed by concrete block
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: The father of a 12-year-old boy who died after being
crushed by a concrete block
has pleaded with the public not to blame his wife or family for the tragedy.
Nor Ismail Nasir, 52, said his eldest son, Mohammad Yusof, had learning disabilities and was hyperactive.
He was working in Pasir Mas when his wife, Nurul Anis Abdul Rahman, 36, called to say their son had been injured.
"When I arrived home, I saw the police. My son was autistic and very active. I cannot say anything more; I accept this as fate," he said when met at Kampung Bukit Gedombak today.
Ismail said his son had never played near the 200kg concrete blocks before.
He admitted the boy was difficult to restrain due to his condition, but maintained that his wife and family had cared for him well.
Recalling the incident, Nurul Anis said she was inside the house with three of her children while Mohammad Yusof and his 10-year-old sister, Siti Nur Amaliana, were playing in the yard with their 12-year-old uncle, Mohd Yunus Nasir.
Siti Nur Amaliana ran into the house to report that the two boys had fallen into the sand and were pinned by a stone block.
"I rushed to the scene and saw my son face down in the sand. I called five or six villagers to help move the concrete block so I could pull him out.
"He was still alive when we got him out and managed to say, 'Mat is going now, I won't be going to school anymore.'
He also told me that if he was gone, I should stay at Moksu Yah's house," she said.
The boy was buried at the Kampung Wakaf Berangan Muslim Cemetery at 1.20am today following a post-mortem at the Tengku Anis Hospital.
The other victim, Mohd Yunus, is currently receiving treatment at Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital in Kota Baru.
Ismail said both victims were special needs students at SK Dalam Rhu.
It was reported yesterday that the victim died after being crushed by a concrete block while digging sand in front of his home in Kampung Telaga Papan, Semerak, at 2.30pm.
Pasir Puteh police chief Superintendent Saari Yaacob had said family members later informed authorities that both boys had fallen into a sand pit before they were found trapped under concrete blocks following sand erosion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Injured wife still unaware husband was killed in home invasion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The family of an
elderly man killed during a break-in robbery
in Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, yesterday has yet to tell his wife about his death.
The victim's eldest son, identified only as Teh, said his mother, who was also attacked in the incident, remains hospitalised and is still unaware that her husband had died, China Press reported.
He said the family feared the news could worsen her condition.
"We still do not dare tell our mother that our father died from his injuries.
"She has asked about his condition, and we only told her that he was injured. We may tell her in one or two days," he said.
Teh was met by reporters at the mortuary of Hospital Raja Permaisuri Bainun while handling procedures to claim his father's body.
In the incident, a burglar broke into the couple's home to steal valuables, resulting in the elderly man's death and serious injuries to his wife.
Teh said his father suffered fatal head injuries, while his right hand was fractured in the attack.
Asked whether police had informed the family of any developments, he said officers had acted swiftly and carried out investigations, but no updates had been provided so far.
The victim's remains will be taken to Ayer Tawar for the wake ceremony.
It was reported earlier that police arrested a 27-year-old suspect, who has been remanded for a week.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia resumes search for three hikers missing after Mount Dukono eruption
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesian authorities resumed search efforts on Saturday for three hikers missing following the eruption of Mount Dukono on Halmahera island, an official said.
Mount Dukono, in North Maluku province, erupted on Friday at 7.41am (2241 GMT Thursday), spewing volcanic ash as high as 10km (6.2 miles) into the sky, the country's volcanology agency said.
The rescue agency had halted the search on Friday evening as it continued to erupt.
At least 100 rescuers, military and police personnel as well as two thermal drones have been deployed since Saturday morning to find the three missing, Iwan Ramdani, the head of the local rescue agency, told Reuters.
The hikers were two Singaporeans and one Indonesian, he added.
"We are focusing the search around the crater, covering an area around 700 metres," Iwan said.
Authorities on Friday had evacuated 17 people, of whom seven were Singaporeans and 10 Indonesians.
Survivors said three people, including two Singaporeans, died in the eruption, local police chief Erlichson Pasaribu had said, but the rescue agency has yet to confirm the deaths as of Saturday.
Saturday's search was still hampered by continued eruptions, Iwan said.
The volcanology agency reported at least four eruptions on Saturday morning.
The volcanology agency is maintaining the alert status of Mount Dukono at the third-highest level, she added.
Residents and tourists were advised not to do any activities within 4 km (2.5 miles) radius from the crater, she said.
There were no reports yet of flight disruptions caused by the eruption.
Indonesia sits on the Pacific "Ring of Fire", an area of high seismic activity atop various tectonic plates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Million-ringgit cars, zero road tax: Offence leaves RTD 'baffled'
Article:
SEREMBAN: Professionals and individuals with 'Datuk' titles are among those caught driving luxury vehicles worth millions of ringgit without valid road tax or insurance.
Negri Sembilan Road Transport Department (RTD) director Jasyindar Singh Sidhu said 143 luxury vehicles were seized in the state in 2025 for the offence.
Between January and April this year, another 45 luxury vehicles were seized for the same offence.
"Among the vehicles seized in 2025 were seven Porsches, 11 Audis, 21 Toyota Alphards and Toyota Vellfires, 30 Mercedes-Benzes, 35 BMWs, and 39 other models.
"From January to April this year, the department seized three Porsches, four Audis, three Toyota Alphards and Vellfires, 15 Mercedes-Benzes, 12 BMWs, and eight other vehicles," he told a press conference.
He said in one case, the RTD seized a car belonging to a Datuk in his late 40s.
The offence, he said, continues to baffle the department.
"They can afford a Porsche but not road tax and insurance. It is baffling," he said.
In a separate matter, Jasyindar said the department seized 335 motorcycles ridden by foreigners in 2025, with another 175 seized between January and April this year.
He said action was taken against 460 foreigners in 2025 and another 101 between January and April this year. Most of the offenders were Pakistanis (224), Indonesians (116), and Bangladeshis (159).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah exco sacked by Bersatu to continue duties for now, says MB
Article:
ALOR STAR: The Kedah government will refer the positions of two assemblymen, one a state exco member and the other a deputy speaker, who were
sacked by Bersatu
yesterday, to the Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said any decision involving Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani and Sungai Tiang assemblyman Abdul Razak Khamis must follow due process and receive the Kedah ruler's consent.
Dzowahir is the state agriculture, plantation and transportation committee chairman (exco), while Abdul Razak is a deputy state assembly speaker.
"We need to discuss this (their positions) in detail, inform the Sultan, and proceed according to established procedures.
"They are still carrying out their respective duties at this time," he said when commenting on the duo's status.
He said both assemblymen continued to support the Perikatan Nasional-led state government.
"They are not the opposition. They are Independent assemblymen who support the state government. Their positions will be discussed further," he said.
He added that the state government had previously received a letter from Bersatu requesting changes to the exco and deputy speaker posts, but no action was taken at the time as both leaders were still party members.
The matter was also discussed with Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin and secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali during the Kubang Rotan state constituency and Kedah Bersatu Aidilfitri open house on April 10.
"Now that official dismissal letters have been received, any action remains subject to the appointment procedures for exco members and the deputy speaker," he said.
Yesterday, Bersatu disciplinary board chairman Datuk Mohd Radzi Manan issued a notice confirming Dzowahir and Abdul Razak's dismissal over a breach of ethics.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar cautious over subsidy cuts, says Mat Sabu
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has made the welfare of the people a top priority, ensuring ministries do not arbitrarily remove subsidies for essential goods and food.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said he and his officers had to meet with Anwar several times to discuss the decision to withdraw the chicken egg subsidy.
He said the prime minister was initially hesitant to agree to the move due to concerns over potential price hikes for the staple item.
"The prime minister is the hardest person to convince when it comes to price increases.
"When we wanted to withdraw the egg subsidy, my officers and I had to meet him repeatedly. He asked, 'Won't the price go up?' He then joked, 'Mat, if it goes up, you're responsible,'" Mohamad said during a speech at the Penang Amanah office in Sungai Dua last night.
He said the subsidy was only removed after he convinced the prime minister that the ministry had held discussions with poultry farmers.
Following the move, prices remained stable, and supply became abundant.
Mohamad said the government cannot be hostile toward farmers, noting that while one or two cartels may act in bad faith, the majority are cooperative.
"Alhamdulillah, three months into the Middle East conflict, there has been no major impact on Malaysia. I visit markets whenever I travel, whether to Indonesia, China, Korea, or Vietnam.
"Food prices globally are rising, but Malaysia remains in a relatively good position. This is what we want. Where the government can provide support, we will," he said.
He also credited the public for following advice not to engage in panic buying of rice, which has helped keep prices under control.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: An undying love
Article:
SHAH ALAM: It has been more than 787 days since Azreein Aziz lost his beloved wife, Nor Hazamiah Lokman, who passed away after suddenly falling ill and slipping into a coma.
Yet, the love he holds for her has not faded, nor has the longing in his heart for his beloved eased.
For Azreein, 50, life has never been the same. His wife's grave has become a place he returns to often - a quiet refuge where memories of love remain alive.
Despite working as an accounts manager in Setia Alam, he continues to find time to visit her final resting place, simply to feel close to her again.
Nor Hazamiah died after a 55-day coma at Universiti Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM), Kuala Lumpur, following a sudden stroke and blood infection.
"She fainted beside me while we were eating. Before that, she said she felt dizzy and her hand was numb. She was rushed to hospital and never woke up again," he said.
For 55 days, Azreein and his three children took turns staying by her side. Eventually, doctors advised that further intervention would not help.
"The doctor asked my permission to 'let her go'," he said quietly. "She passed away beside me. I was also the one who bathed and buried her."
"I brushed her cheek for the last time before she was laid to rest.
"For the first 500 days, I had the opportunity to visit my late wife's grave every day. But now, I visit her grave every other day or on weekends due to work commitments. I am grateful that Allah SWT still grants me 'rezeki' and the opportunity to visit my late wife's resting place," he said.
Their love story began in 1994, after SPM. She was his high school sweetheart.
"I was not well-off, just an ordinary man, but she accepted me. We slept on the floor during the first year of our marriage."
"We never had a honeymoon then. But after 16 years, I managed to take her to Istanbul. She always loved to watch shows from there," he added.
Even now, Azreein believes she has never truly left him.
He said he has shared his story not to seek attention, but to remind others of something simple yet profound.
"When I shared it on Threads before, it was simply so people would know that visiting graves can revive a heart that has long been spiritually numb. Since I began frequently visiting my wife's grave, I have become closer to the Quran and my recitation has improved," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 44 evacuated as flash floods hit Bandar Baharu
Article:
BANDAR BAHARU: Forty-four flash flood victims from 12 families have been evacuated to the Dewan Keda Sungai Tengas temporary relief centre (PPS) following heavy rain last night.
Bandar Baharu Civil Defence Force officer Lieutenant Abdul Rahim Khairuddin said the figures, recorded as of 7.30am today, involve residents from Kampung Sungai Tengas.
The victims include 14 men, 14 women, nine children, three infants, and four senior citizens.
"They moved to the PPS starting at 8.30pm last night. It is still raining," he said in a statement today.
He said heavy rain lasting more than two hours yesterday caused five villages across four townships — Sungai Batu, Selama, Bagan Samak, and Serdang — to be inundated.
The affected villages are Kampung Sungai Tengas, Kampung Air Itam Dalam, Kampung Cherok Meranti, Kampung Paya Salak, and Kampung Batu Hampar.
Rahim added that the water level of Sungai Terunsing in Kampung Sungai Tengas has reached the danger level.
According to the Drainage and Irrigation Department's Public Infobanjir website, the level of Sungai Titi Kerbau in Kubur Panjang, Pendang, also surpassed the danger mark at 7am today.
Three other rivers in Baling, Sik, and Kubang Pasu have exceeded the alert level.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-lawyer liable for misappropriating RM415,000 from senior citizen property sale, court rules
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court has ordered a former lawyer to pay RM415,000 to a 73-year-old woman after finding that he had misappropriated funds in relation to proceeds from a property sale transaction involving a Cheras condominium.
Judge Effandi Nazila Abdullah said Na Wee Jern, who ran the law firm Wee Jern & Partners, failed to remit the sale proceeds to the plaintiff despite receiving the sale proceeds of the apartment.
The court was told that the buyer had deposited about RM415,000 into the law firm's account as payment for the purchase of the Cheras property. However, Tan did not receive any of the money.
Instead, the judge said that the defendant gave inconsistent explanations and made false representations regarding key transaction timelines in an attempt to justify the delay in payment.
In written grounds of judgment, Effandi said: "On the balance of probabilities, this court allows the plaintiff's claim."
"The court agrees with the plaintiff's submission that the defendant as the appointed solicitor, has breached fiduciary responsibility toward the plaintiff when the defendant failed to release 90 per cent of the sale proceeds to the plaintiff according to the terms of the said SPA," the judge said.
The judge said the defendant had a fiduciary responsibility toward the plaintiff (Tan) because the defendant (Na) acted as the sole trustee who received and kept any sale proceeds on behalf of the Plaintiff in the sale and purchase transaction of the said Property.
The court added that Tan had suffered financial loss as a result of the defendant's failure to remit the sale proceeds and allowed the claim with costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel fraud risks carry severe penalties under Customs Act, warns lawyer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — False declarations, manipulated documents, or undocumented fuel movements could expose companies and individuals to severe penalties under the Customs Act 1967, cautioned Admiralty and Maritime lawyer Datuk J. Shamesh.
He was commenting on recent concerns raised by an industry player regarding revenue leakage and documentation gaps in Malaysia’s petroleum supply chain.
He highlighted that several provisions under the Act could apply, including Section 133, which covers false declarations, inaccurate statements and the submission of incorrect particulars, with penalties of up to RM500,000 in fines, seven years’ imprisonment, or both.
“In addition, Section 135 deals with the fraudulent evasion of customs duties or prohibition controls, while Section 138 concerns offences linked to the creation or use of false documents.
“Section 147 extends liability to agents, employees or company officers in cases where offences are committed by corporations,” he said, adding that those found liable face varying fines and imprisonment.
Shamesh added that irregularities involving subsidised fuel, excise duties, or sales tax liabilities could also be subject to scrutiny under other relevant legislation, depending on the specific circumstances.
Regulators would typically determine whether discrepancies arose from operational issues or deliberate misconduct by examining the frequency and pattern of inconsistencies, he explained.
“This includes determining whether the discrepancies repeatedly resulted in reduced revenue exposure, reviewing the integrity of operational records and audit trails, identifying unexplained stock gains or losses, and assessing whether there is evidence of concealment or falsification.
“Minor isolated variances can occur due to legitimate operational factors such as temperature adjustments, meter tolerances or tank calibration differences.
“However, recurring inconsistencies, unexplained blending activities, manipulated density readings or discrepancies unsupported by proper contemporaneous records may point towards possible revenue leakage or fraudulent declarations,” he emphasised.
Shamesh noted that effective regulatory reforms could include mandatory real-time inventory reconciliation, tighter licensing controls over bonded petroleum facilities, enhanced independent calibration and verification requirements, periodic forensic stock audits, and stronger inter-agency coordination between customs, maritime, tax and energy regulators.
He said such reforms should focus on risk-based enforcement and targeted transparency measures, without unnecessarily disrupting legitimate industry operations or fuel supply chains. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia’s airspace stays resilient amid rising regional flight traffic, says CAAM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Malaysia’s airspace operations remain resilient in handling high volumes of regional air traffic, underscoring the country’s role as a key aviation connectivity hub in Southeast Asia, according to the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM).
In a statement today, CAAM said it continues to strengthen airspace efficiency within the Kuala Lumpur Flight Information Region (KL FIR) and Kota Kinabalu Flight Information Region (KK FIR) amid evolving global airspace dynamics and increasing operational complexity.
Regional traffic data showed that Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) remains among the busiest airports in Southeast Asia, supported by several of the region’s most heavily travelled routes.
CAAM said key corridors such as Kuala Lumpur-Singapore, Jakarta-Kuala Lumpur and Kota Kinabalu-Kuala Lumpur continue to record high flight frequencies.
“This underscores Malaysia’s strategic role in maintaining regional connectivity and ensuring the continuity of air travel across Asean,” it said.
The authority said continuous improvements in airspace design and air traffic management have enabled both KL FIR and KK FIR to accommodate growing traffic volumes while maintaining high standards of safety, efficiency and reliability.
These include the implementation of Required Navigation Performance Approach (RNP APCH) procedures at KLIA, as well as full Performance-Based Navigation (PBN) Standard Instrument Departure (SID), Standard Terminal Arrival Route (STAR) and RNP APCH procedures in the Kota Kinabalu FIR.
CAAM said the measures enable more precise routing, reduced track miles and improved arrival and departure efficiency.
It added that the expansion of Direct Route Operations (DRO) and User Preferred Routes (UPR) allows aircraft to operate on more efficient and flexible flight paths, reducing congestion, flight times and fuel consumption.
Enhanced cross-boundary coordination also supports more adaptive routing in response to changing operational conditions.
CAAM said the adoption of advanced air traffic management concepts such as System Wide Information Management (SWIM) and Flight and Flow Information for a Collaborative Environment (FF-ICE) enables real-time data sharing, improves predictability and supports more effective collaborative decision-making across the aviation ecosystem.
Advancements in Performance-Based Communication and Surveillance (PBCS) have also enhanced operational capacity by enabling more efficient aircraft separation while maintaining high safety standards, it said.
It further noted the efficiency of KL FIR and KK FIR as critical to supporting one of Southeast Asia’s busiest aviation networks, with KL FIR serving as a central node for major regional routes and KK FIR strengthening connectivity across East Malaysia while supporting growing cross-border traffic flows. — Bernama

Title: Kedah govt to seek Sultan’s consent over fate of two Bersatu-sacked reps
Article:
ALOR STAR, May 9 — The Kedah government will refer the positions of two state assemblymen — one a state executive councillor and the other a deputy speaker — sacked by Bersatu yesterday to the Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah,
New Straits Times (NST)
reported.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said any decision involving Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani, who is state agriculture, plantation and transportation committee chairman, and Sungai Tiang assemblyman Abdul Razak Khamis, who is a deputy state assembly speaker, must follow due process and receive the consent of the Kedah ruler.
“We need to discuss this in detail, inform the Sultan, and proceed according to established procedures.
“They are still carrying out their respective duties at this time,” he was quoted as saying.
Sanusi said both assemblymen continued to support the Perikatan Nasional-led state government and were not in the opposition.
“They are Independent assemblymen who support the state government. Their positions will be discussed further,” he reportedly said.
He added that the state government had previously received a letter from Bersatu requesting changes to the exco and deputy speaker posts, but no action was taken then as both were still party members.
Sanusi said the matter was also discussed with Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin and secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali during the Kubang Rotan state constituency and Kedah Bersatu Aidilfitri open house on April 10.
“Now that official dismissal letters have been received, any action remains subject to the appointment procedures for exco members and the deputy speaker,” he was quoted as saying.
Yesterday, Bersatu disciplinary board chairman Datuk Mohd Radzi Manan issued a notice confirming the dismissal of Dzowahir and Abdul Razak over alleged breaches of ethics.

Title: Communications Ministry steps in to train small traders at Melaka central market
Article:
MELAKA, May 9 — The Communications Ministry will channel allocations to the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) and the Madani Community to carry out targeted outreach programmes for traders at the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB) Central Market in Bachang here.
Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said his visit today found that many traders had requested classes on online sales, as well as assistance with applications for the Madani Subsidy Assistance Programme (BUDI) Individual for diesel.
“For small traders, some may not know how to apply for BUDI Individual. This is among the areas that need to be strengthened, including through the Madani Community.
“Entrepreneurship is one of the five main pillars at Nadi, including online sales, so we have asked Nadi to provide basic training for the traders here,” he told reporters when attending the Jiwa@Komuniti Madani Programme: Sembang Santai Parlimen Hang Tuah Jaya at the market.
Also present were Communications Ministry deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) Nik Kamaruzaman Nik Husin and Information Department director-general Julina Johan.
At the programme, Fahmi spent more than two hours engaging with traders at food stalls, as well as those selling wet and dry goods. — Bernama

Title: Army deploys drones, ATVs and scramblers along Malaysia’s border ahead of Aidiladha to curb smuggling
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 9 — The Malaysian Army (TDM) is tightening security along the country’s borders to curb smuggling and cross-border crimes ahead of the Aidiladha celebrations later this month.
Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said the force remains on constant alert to ensure the continued security and sovereignty of the nation’s borders.
“We will further tighten control at every border, especially along the northern border between Malaysia and Thailand.
“It is during festive periods like this that activities violating regulations tend to occur,” he told reporters after officiating the Army Run 2026 in conjunction with the 93rd Army Day celebration here today.
According to Azhan, existing operations along the country’s borders will continue to ensure there are no security breaches at the nation’s entry points.
Earlier, Azhan said the army would continue strengthening the deployment of personnel and assets along the country’s borders to ensure border security remains safeguarded and under control.
He said the effort is being supported by the latest technology, including the use of drones, scrambler motorcycles, all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and optronic equipment.
The media previously reported that the army successfully foiled an attempt to smuggle 20 cattle from Thailand, estimated to be worth RM225,000, during an operation at the border on May 4.
On Army Run 2026, Azhan said the event received an encouraging response, with the participation of 6,000 people comprising members of the Malaysian Armed Forces, government agencies, the private sector, amateur athletes and the public from across the country.
“Insya-Allah, we will organise this event again next year,” he said.
Meanwhile, the army, in a statement, said the inaugural Army Run 2026 reflected the close unity between army personnel and the public, in line with the Whole of Government and Whole of Society (WoGoS) approach in strengthening cooperation for the nation’s interests.
“Besides promoting a healthy and active lifestyle, the programme also serves as a platform to strengthen ties between the army and the people through community participation in a safe, organised and patriotic environment,” it said. — Bernama

Title: ‘Brave’ joins Bomba’s K9 unit as Putrajaya pumps RM2.4m to expand tracker dog force
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 9 — The K9 Tracker Dog Unit of the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) has welcomed a new ‘member’ after its Labrador, Lottie, gave birth to a male puppy on March 18.
Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the puppy, born to the dog under the care of fire officer Nelson Karupak, has been named ‘Brave’.
“I named this puppy ‘Brave’ in the hope that it will grow into a courageous and resilient four-legged hero in carrying out rescue duties,” he said when launching the World Firefighters’ Day Carnival 2026, themed ‘Bomba Tonggak Penyelamatan Negara’, at Plaza Angsana here today.
Also present were Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Aiman Athirah Sabu and JBPM director-general Datuk Nor Hisham Mohammad.
He said JBPM currently has only 34 tracker dogs and requires a further 56 to reach optimal operational capacity.
Recognising the importance of the K9 unit, Nga said the Madani Government has approved an allocation of RM2.4 million to procure 16 new tracker dogs from the United Kingdom.
As a long-term strategic measure, he said JBPM is also planning to develop a K9 Operations Centre in Bertam, Penang, which will implement a domestic breeding programme.
The two-day World Firefighters’ Day 2026 celebration, is organised in conjunction with the 50th anniversary of the department.
The event is also enlivened by a static parade involving 550 participants and 50 fire engines and vehicles, including several belonging to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. — Bernama

Title: Need help getting around? Rapid KL rolls out ‘Ask Me’ helpers to guide commuters, starting at Pasar Seni
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Rapid KL has introduced the “Ask Me” customer assistance initiative at the Pasar Seni Bus Hub as the first location for its implementation in an effort to enhance the public transport travel experience through faster, clearer and more accessible information delivery.
Rapid KL said in a statement today that the initiative was introduced to help passengers obtain travel-related assistance more easily, particularly at bus hubs with high commuter traffic and multiple route connections.
According to the bus service operator, a special “Ask Me” podium will be placed at the hub to make it easier for passengers to identify where assistance is available.
“Passengers can obtain information on bus routes, stop locations, route changes, travel planning as well as travel pass products to help facilitate their daily journeys.
“This initiative is part of the Service Ambassador 2.0 Programme, which features Customer Service Assistants and Traffic Coordinators as the main personnel at key hotspots and major transport hubs,” the statement said.
Rapid Bus acting chief executive officer Ku Jamil Zakaria said the implementation of “Ask Me” reflects Rapid KL’s ongoing commitment to making public transport more responsive and reliable for users.
He said the Pasar Seni Bus Hub was selected as the first location because it is among the main bus hubs in the Klang Valley, handling 14 Rapid KL bus routes with an average of more than 6,000 passengers daily.
He said the implementation of “Ask Me” will also be expanded in stages to other locations based on operational needs and suitability, including at the Weld Quay Bus Terminal for Rapid Penang services.
“We want to ensure passengers can obtain assistance more quickly and easily, especially at locations that serve as key interchange points in their daily journeys.
“The presence of easily identifiable personnel not only helps reduce confusion, but also boosts users’ confidence when using public transport,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Zahid says BN cooperation in unity govt driven by shared goals, not political complacency
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) are comfortable working with all component parties in the Unity Government, as they share common objectives, particularly in serving the interests of the people, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said the ease of cooperation that has emerged does not indicate complacency, but is instead the result of strong understanding among parties in the government to ensure the people are not burdened by political polemics.
“The people must not be troubled by prolonged polemics and differences in stance or views,” the Deputy Prime Minister said in a special interview held in conjunction with Umno’s 80th anniversary recently.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also the BN chairman, said that in the interest of the people, all parties in the Unity Government have agreed to set aside differences and political considerations to ensure the effective implementation of every agenda outlined in the 15th General Election (GE15) manifesto.
He said the government gave priority to common ground in the manifestos of parties contesting GE15, particularly on issues related to the cost of living, education and unemployment.
“On matters concerning education and unemployment, there is no disagreement between the competing parties,” said Ahmad Zahid, who is also the Rural and Regional Development Minister.
He also said that 47 per cent of the manifestos of parties in the Unity Government had common elements, thereby forming a strong foundation for existing cooperation.
“Why should we be concerned about differences when we share the same goal?
“In addition, there are detailed issues that we must address. That is why we have the Inter-Party Consultative Council, to resolve differences and ensure the manifesto is implemented with clear purpose,” he said.
In the meantime, Ahmad Zahid emphasised that all component parties in the Unity Government, including DAP, uphold the four main pillars enshrined in the Federal Constitution.
